sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board looks the same. Let me check what changed. I need to compare the before/after frames to see what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

before_map = {}
for n in before['nodes']:
    before_map[n['id']] = n

after_map = {}
for n in after['nodes']:
    after_map[n['id']] = n

all_keys = set(list(before_map.keys()) + list(after_map.keys()))

for nid in all_keys:
    b = before_map.get(nid)
    a = after_map.get(nid)
    if b and a:
        if b['color'] != a['color'] or b['pixels'] != a['pixels']:
            print("Node %d: %s->%s, pix %d->%d" % (nid, b['color'], a['color'], b['pixels'], a['pixels']))
    elif b and not a:
        print("Node removed: %d %s" % (nid, b['color']))
    elif not b and a:
        print("Node added: %d %s" % (nid, a['color']))
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"action_semantics": {}, "obj_tax": {}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "timer/progress bar at row 63"}, "win_pat": {}, "lvl_log": {}, "cross_lvl": {}, "wm": {}, "gm": {}, "rf": {}, "oq": {}, "cp": {}, "sl": {}, "fp": {}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Interesting! The green rectangle (node 2) went from 5 pixels back to 14 pixels. So clicking on the other C shape (node 14) "undid" the previous click! This is a toggle behavior!
So clicking on C shape (node 13) toggles the green rectangle from 14 to 5 pixels. Clicking on the C shape (node 14) toggles the green rectangle back from 5 to 14 pixels.
So the game is about toggling the green rectangle between 14 and 5 pixels.
Wait, but the C shape (node 13) toggles the green rectangle between 14 and 5 pixels. Clicking on the other C shape (node 14) toggles it back from 5 to 14 pixels.
So the C shapes are toggles!
So the game is about toggling the green rectangle between 14 and 5 pixels.
Wait, but the C shapes (nodes 13 and 14) are the C shapes inside the gray frame. Clicking on one toggles the green rectangle (node 2) between 14 and 5 pixels. Clicking on the other C shape (node 14) toggles it back from 5 to 14 pixels.
So the C shapes are toggles!
Now let me think about what the goal is. The game has:
- Two C shapes (nodes 13 and 14) that toggle the green rectangle (node 2) between 14 and 5 pixels.
- Two U shapes (nodes 20 and 23) that probably toggle the yellow rectangle (node 16) between some values.
- Two cross shapes (nodes 3 and 24) that probably toggle something else.
Let me try clicking on the U shapes to see if they toggle the yellow rectangle.
